message.content:
I'm planning implementation for an interview skeleton project at c:\Users\Basyc\Desktop\test\interview-ai-fullstack-v1.

Explore Module E (Docker + Git) and overall project config. Read these files fully:
- docker-compose.yml (report every TODO marker and current content)
- Dockerfile (backend, if exists)
- frontend/Dockerfile (report if missing)
- .env.example (note: git status shows it deleted — check if it exists)
- package.json (root) and frontend/package.json — scripts, deps
- pnpm-workspace.yaml
- frontend/next.config.js
- .gitignore
- SELF-REVIEW.template.md
- Any existing .claude/ directory contents

Report the docker-compose service definitions needed (backend, frontend, mongodb, redis), what healthcheck/depends_on/volumes are missing, and what frontend/Dockerfile needs. Report file paths with line numbers. Return only conclusions needed to write an implementation plan.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- claude-md-management:revise-claude-md
- claude-md-management:claude-md-improver
- document-skills:xlsx
- document-skills:docx
- document-skills:pptx
- document-skills:pdf
- frontend-design:frontend-design
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init
- review
- security-review

12	  },
13	  "scripts": {
14	    "start": "pm2 start ecosystem.config.json --no-daemon",
15	    "dev": "cross-env NODE_ENV=development nodemon src/index.js",
16	    "test": "jest -i --colors --verbose --detectOpenHandles",
17	    "test:watch": "jest -i --watchAll",
18	    "coverage": "jest -i --coverage",

content.text:
Exploration complete. Here are the conclusions for the implementation plan.

## Module E scope (from TASK.md:19-20)
- E "Docker + Git" (branch `chore/compose`, 10 pts): create `frontend/Dockerfile`; complete four-service `healthcheck` / `depends_on` / `volumes`; semantic commits.
- Separately (10 pts): `.claude/` must contain session records and be committed; `SELF-REVIEW.md` four questions answered.
- Target UX (TASK.md:32-35): `docker compose up -d` → backend on :3000, frontend on :3001.

## docker-compose.yml — `c:\Users\Basyc\Desktop\test\interview-ai-fullstack-v1\docker-compose.yml`
Current: 4 services on bridge network `app-network`, `restart: unless-stopped`, no volumes/healthchecks/depends_on. Every TODO marker:
- Line 12: backend — add `depends_on` mongodb + redis with `condition: service_healthy`.
- Line 20: frontend build references `Dockerfile` that does not exist yet (`# TODO: 创建 frontend/Dockerfile`).
- Line 23: frontend — add `NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL` / `NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL` env vars.
- Line 24: frontend — add `depends_on: backend`.
- Line 33: mongodb — add volume persistence.
- Line 36: mongodb — add healthcheck `mongosh --eval 'db.adminCommand("ping")'`.
- Line 43: redis — add volume persistence.
- Line 46: redis — add healthcheck `redis-cli ping`.
- Line 49: define top-level `volumes` (`mongodb_data` / `redis_data`); note there is NO top-level `volumes:` key currently, only `networks:` at line 51-53.

Existing wiring: backend uses `env_file: - .env` (line 10-11), ports backend `3000:3000` (line 8-9), frontend `3001:3001` (line 21-22), mongodb `27017`, redis `6379`.

## Backend Dockerfile — `...\Dockerfile` (broken, needs rework)
Lines 1-16 are Yarn-based and incomplete:
- Line 1: `FROM node:alpine` (unpinned).
- Line 7: `COPY package.json yarn.lock ./` but repo uses pnpm (`pnpm-lock.yaml` exists, no `yarn.lock`).
- Line 11: `RUN yarn install --pure-lockfile` — will fail (no yarn.lock).
- Line 15: `EXPOSE 3000` only. No `CMD`/start command, no build of app. Needs conversion to pnpm (`corepack enable` + `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`) and a start command (`node src/index.js`, or `pnpm start` which invokes pm2 per package.json:14).

## frontend/Dockerfile — MISSING
Confirmed absent (`ls frontend/Dockerfile` → not found). Must be created. Needs: Node base, pnpm install, `next build`, expose 3001, `next start -p 3001`. frontend/package.json (`...\frontend\package.json`) scripts: `dev`=`next dev -p 3001`, `build`=`next build`, `start`=`next start -p 3001`; deps next 14.2.3 / react 18.3.1. A standalone/output config is NOT set in next.config.js (`...\frontend\next.config.js` only has `reactStrictMode: true`), so a plain build+`next start` image is simplest (no `output: 'standalone'`).

## .env / .env.example
- `.env.example` is git-tracked but DELETED in the working tree (git status `D .env.example`); on-disk `ls` confirms it is gone. Content still recoverable from HEAD:
  - `NODE_ENV=development`, `PORT=3000`, `MONGODB_URL=mongodb://mongodb:27017/interview`, `REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379`, `JWT_SECRET=interview_secret_change_me`, `JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION_MINUTES=120`, `JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION_DAYS=1`.
  - The URLs already use Docker service hostnames (`mongodb`, `redis`), so they work inside compose. Plan should restore/recreate `.env.example` and have user `cp .env.example .env` (TASK.md:30). backend reads `.env` via `env_file` and config.js:5.
- Required backend env vars (config.js:9-15, Joi-validated, `.required()`): `NODE_ENV`, `MONGODB_URL`, `REDIS_URL`, `JWT_SECRET` (PORT defaults 3000).
- `frontend/.env.local.example` (`...\frontend\.env.local.example`): `NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:3000`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL=ws://localhost:3000` — these are the values for the compose frontend env (line 23 TODO); note `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` are build-time-inlined for Next, so they belong as build `args`/env at build time, not just runtime.

## Package / workspace config
- Root `package.json` (`...\package.json`): backend. `start`=`pm2 start ecosystem.config.json --no-daemon` (ecosystem.config.json runs `src/index.js`, single instance). `dev`=nodemon. Docker scripts (lines 24-26) reference `docker-compose.prod/dev/test.yml`. Deps include bullmq, ioredis, ws, mongoose 8. Server binds `app.listen(config.port)` at src/index.js:19. No `/health` HTTP endpoint exists (grep found none) — backend healthcheck cannot rely on an HTTP health route unless one is added; consider tcp/port check or add endpoint.
- `pnpm-workspace.yaml` (`...\pnpm-workspace.yaml`) is UNTRACKED (git status `??`) and contains only `allowBuilds:` (scarf/msgpackr-extract) — it does NOT declare `packages:`, so frontend is effectively a separate install, not a linked workspace. Both root and `frontend/` have their own `pnpm-lock.yaml`.
- `docker-compose.dev.yml` / `docker-compose.prod.yml` (`...\docker-compose.dev.yml`, `...\docker-compose.prod.yml`) still reference an old `node-app` service with `yarn dev`/`yarn start` — these do NOT match the current `backend` service name in docker-compose.yml and are stale (potential cleanup or override-name fix).

## Other config
- `.gitignore` (`...\.gitignore`): ignores `node_modules`, `.env*` but keeps `!.env*.example`. So a recreated `.env.example` will be tracked correctly.
- `.dockerignore` (`...\.dockerignore`): only `node_modules`, `.git`, `.gitignore` — minimal; fine but does not exclude `.env`, tests, etc.
- `.claude/` (`...\.claude\`): contains only `.gitkeep` (tracked). CLAUDE.md is UNTRACKED at repo root (git status `??`). Process-evidence requirement (TASK.md:20) not yet satisfied — session records need to be added and committed.
- `SELF-REVIEW.template.md` (`...\SELF-REVIEW.template.md`): template present; a filled `SELF-REVIEW.md` does not yet exist. Line 15 explicitly lists Module E acceptance: "frontend/Dockerfile + 四服务 healthcheck / depends_on / volumes".

## Note / gotcha for the plan
There is a separate `workers/jobWorker.js` (Module C BullMQ worker) not covered by the 4 compose services and not in ecosystem.config.json. TASK.md Module E only mandates the four services (backend, frontend, mongodb, redis), so the worker either runs inside the backend process or is out of Module E scope — flag this as a decision point.
